summary: Resolve the M6 Color-versus-Base-Material specification contradiction as amendment A-11 and correct the fresh-profile Welcome instruction so it names the Open project action actually available on screen.

providedInputs.label: Owner direction to fix the Q-0042 contradiction, correct the first-run copy defect, and preserve strict-zero gating

decisions.decision: Amend FR-15.15 and AC-15.k to require the pinned engine's standard Materials-extension Color encoding: two object meshes, one colorgroup with distinct color entries, object triangle/effective-property references that resolve to the intended colors, and no basematerials group.
decisions.rationale: FR-15.14 and A-7 already prohibit Base Material because the pinned lazy-union engine path assigns the first color to every object in that mode. The exact-engine probe and shipped implementation use Color encoding successfully, so the older basematerials/displaycolor acceptance wording was internally contradictory and false.

decisions.decision: Say 'open a project' in the Welcome description and retain 'reopen' only for conditional recent-project buttons that actually exist.
decisions.rationale: A genuinely fresh profile has an Open project action and no Reopen action. Naming the available control keeps first-run instructions literal without changing behavior.

testEvidence.criterion: The normative M6 Color format must be internally consistent across FR-15.15, AC-15.k, the question queue, and current release notes.
testEvidence.test: Require colorgroup/color wording and explicit Base Material rejection in the two spec sections, A-11 in the amendment log, Q-0042 resolved, and the current release notes labeled as a resolved correction.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/scripts/release-metadata.test.ts -t "keeps the normative M6 color format internally consistent"
red.outcome: RED: 1 test file failed; 1 test failed and 3 were skipped. FR-15.15 still required basematerials/displaycolor and contained no colorgroup requirement.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/welcome/WelcomeLauncher.test.tsx tests/scripts/release-metadata.test.ts
green.outcome: GREEN: 2 test files passed; all 8 tests passed.

testEvidence.criterion: A fresh-profile Welcome instruction must name the Open project action that is visible and must not imply a Reopen action before recent-project history exists.
testEvidence.test: Render the Welcome dialog with no recent projects, require the corrected accessible description, require the Open project button, and require no Reopen button.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/welcome/WelcomeLauncher.test.tsx -t "contains focus in the active dialog"
red.outcome: RED: 1 test file failed; 1 test failed and 3 were skipped. The dialog said 'reopen a project' while the fresh surface exposed only Open project.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/welcome/WelcomeLauncher.test.tsx tests/scripts/release-metadata.test.ts
green.outcome: GREEN: 2 test files passed; all 8 tests passed.

nearMisses.sought: Reject the obsolete Base Material requirement without rejecting the new explicit prohibition of Base Material.
nearMisses.confirmation: The first GREEN attempt correctly changed the spec but the new test matched the prohibited token regardless of context. The assertion was narrowed to require the prohibition and reject displaycolor; a second formatting-only correction made it whitespace-insensitive before the focused suite passed.

attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
